findings.evidence: Line 75 `private noncomputable def antipodeComp`; line 94-95 public `lemma convInv_def ... = toConv (antipodeComp f.ofConv)`.
findings.fix: Mark `convInv_def` `private` (it is only used internally by `convInv_mul_cancel`), leaving `convInv_apply` as the public characterization; or, if a function-level inverse name is wanted, expose `antipodeComp` as a named public def (e.g. `AlgHom.postcompAntipode`) with its own API and state convInv_def in terms of it.
findings.issue: convInv_def is a public lemma whose RHS `toConv (antipodeComp f.ofConv)` names the private `antipodeComp` (line 75). Downstream code cannot reference `antipodeComp`, so this `_def` lemma yields a term with an inaccessible constant — it is effectively dead/misleading public API; the usable characterization is `convInv_apply`.

summary: The characteristic API for the new group structure is complete and well-annotated (convInv_apply @[simp], group instances, mapValue functoriality), and bodies are correctly hidden. One defect: the public convInv_def is stated in terms of the private antipodeComp, making the function-level definitional lemma unusable by downstream.
